reasoning: The overarching factor for the SONIA rate during this forecast period is the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting which concludes on March 19, 2026 (falling conveniently between the comparison dates of March 15 and March 22). Current market pricing reflects approximately a 15-20% chance of a 25 bps rate cut down to 3.50%, with an 80-85% chance of a hold at 3.75%, following recent inflation stickiness related to Middle East conflicts which caused markets to pare back earlier cut expectations. If a cut occurs, SONIA will invariably plummet by ~25 bps, guaranteeing a 'NO' resolution. Should the BoE hold rates, the Daily SONIA will revert to its intrinsic random walk bounded loosely by the 3.75% Bank Rate. Looking at its latest trajectory since early March, SONIA currently resides relatively high at 3.7295; while there is a slight structural upward trend in its spread to the Bank Rate, purely statistical random walk mechanics project roughly a 50% probability that its value on March 20 (the proxy for March 22) will be strictly higher than 3.7295. Adjusting slightly for quarter-end liquidity dynamics that might modestly squeeze the overnight spread tighter, I gauge the 'Hold + Increase' probability to be ~50%. Combining these paths mathematically yields P(Hold)*P(Increase | Hold) = (0.85) * (0.50) ≈ 0.43.
